meta.setting: Long trails, pack camps, stock roads, pine passes, and county edges around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with travel, camp survival, animal handling, informal justice, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Deer sign and prey context for trail work.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Bear sign and camp-food pressure in forest travel.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional roads, mining communities, logging, ranching, and frontier work.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Rangers and Trailhands cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented trail note, camp warning, or horse-sense saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Big-Country Working Riders
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing range riders, trailhands, deputy rangers, and practical guides who keep routes alive without owning them
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
12 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate them as a broad playable archetype for travel, camp, animals, weather, and informal justice.
Tie future moves to horse care, tracking, camp discipline, food, weather, route repair, animal conflict, witness handling, spare mounts, dry matches, and getting people home.
Keep them capable but limited by fatigue, supply, local politics, and the fact that the road belongs to people before it belongs to a map.
Make them useful to player characters without turning them into perfect guides or frontier superheroes.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: They should feel like the people every polished man hires and then ignores until the road vanishes. A trailhand knows the country by the problems it leaves on his clothes.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A trailhand scene should ask what the horses need, what the camp forgot, what the weather changed, and who is responsible for the person lagging behind.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western trail, camp, horse, and ranger language only.
Keep capability practical, fallible, and tied to supply and weather.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/rangers-and-trailhands.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not make them flawless heroes, elite commandos, or generic lawmen
do not use modern slang, vehicles, plastic gear, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence and includes 12 future H4 move slots.
The future page is about travel, camp survival, animal handling, informal justice, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
